📚 Quick Reference
Where to find what you need in the app
Weekly view
Go to Weekly Development — select any agent to see their last 4 weeks of CSAT, SQS, AHT and more.
Monthly view
Go to Monthly Development — full table of all agents rolled up month by month.
QA audits
Go to Support Quality Score — every QA audit per agent with SQS breakdown.
CSAT cases
Go to Your Team CSAT — case-level CSAT data with customer comments and DSAT cases.
Team KPIs
Go to Team Performance — your full team side by side for the last 6 weeks, with threshold flags.
Backup coverage
Go to All Teams — every agent across all teams, including those not in your roster.
Log feedback
Go to Weekly Development — select the agent, then click the green Add/see a review button on the relevant week row.
View feedback history
Go to Feedback Tracker — a read-only record of all feedback entries you have logged.
Action plans
Use Register an Action — log agreed development tasks with due dates and status.
Review actions
Go to Registered Actions Board — kanban view of all open actions per agent.
Due dates
Go to Registered Actions Calendar — see when all actions are due across your team.
Report issue
Not active yet. Contact Evan Dharmasena directly for all app issues during the integration phase.

📚 Quick Reference panel: At any point in this training — or when you're using the app — click the Quick Reference button in the top bar. It gives you instant reminders of where to find anything in the app without having to scroll back through the training.
💡 Good to know: This app works alongside the GSO Reviewers App your agents use. When you log feedback or create an action plan, your agent sees it automatically — no copy-pasting, no chasing.
Next up
Section 01
What Is This App & Why Does It Exist? ⏱ 3 min

The GSO Feedback App was built to give you one place to manage coaching, feedback, and agent development — without the manual admin.

Before this app, logging feedback meant updating a spreadsheet, sending a Google Chat, and hoping the agent saw it. Action plans lived in documents nobody tracked. That changes now.
Before
Manual & scattered
Spreadsheets, Google Chats, scattered docs. No single view of agent development or whether action plans were followed through.
After
Centralised & automatic
One app. Feedback, action plans, and performance data — updated automatically and visible to both you and your agents.
⚠️ Important: This app replaces your manual spreadsheet workflows. Data updates automatically. Check with your manager or Transformation Lead (Evan Dharmasena) if there are any transitional steps needed during go-live.
Next up
Section 02
What You Can See & Do ⏱ 10 min

Every feature in the app — what it does and what it looks like.

Your home screen. One-click access to every section of the app, plus the latest data update date at a glance.
📈
Weekly Development
Your go-to view for individual agent performance — and where you create feedback. Select any agent to see their last 4 weeks of CSAT, SQS, AHT, IRT Chat, Assignable and OBC and more. Each week row has a green Add/see a review button — click it to log Operational Feedback (KPI observations and coaching notes) and Quality Feedback (your assessment of the quality of the agent's work) for that week. Data updates automatically, no manual refresh needed.
Left: the green button on each week row. Right: the feedback form — fill in Operational Feedback, Quality Feedback, then choose whether to send it as an email.
📅
Monthly Development
A broader view of all your agents across months. Good for review cycles and spotting agents whose performance is drifting over a longer period. Expand any agent row to go deeper.
⭐
Support Quality Score
Shows QA audit results per agent — the overall score plus individual scores for each quality dimension. Results flow here automatically from the QA team's audits. This tab does not replace the QMS tool. Team Leaders still need to review their agents' audits in QMS as normal — this view gives you a consolidated reference inside the app, not a replacement for that process.
From QA
😊
Your Team CSAT
Case-level CSAT — every case where a customer gave a low score, with their exact comment and case details. Filter by agent before a coaching session. This is what turns a DSAT number into a specific, evidence-based conversation.
👥
Team Performance
Your whole team's KPIs side by side, week by week. Any metric below threshold is highlighted in red. Use this to spot patterns across the team — which agents are consistently struggling and which KPIs are trending the wrong way.
🏢
All Teams
The same performance view, but for every agent across every team — not just your own. If you're covering for another Team Leader, this is where you'll find those agents. They won't appear anywhere else in the app.
Backup Team Leader
💬
Feedback Tracker
A record of all the feedback you have logged for your agents. Feedback is created from Weekly Development using the green Add/see a review button — the Feedback Tracker is where you view and track those entries after the fact. Each entry shows the week, the agent, Operational Feedback, Quality Feedback, whether it was sent by email, and when it was created. Agents see the same entries in their own app — read-only.
✏️
Register an Action
Creates a development action plan for a specific agent. Choose the task type, set a start and due date, pick the status, and describe the agreed activity. As soon as you save, it appears in the agent's app. Only log what was genuinely agreed in the coaching session — agents see everything immediately.
📋
Registered Actions Board
A kanban-style view of all action plans for a selected agent, sorted by status: Not Started, In Progress, Completed. Add new actions or edit existing ones directly from here. Use it during coaching check-ins to review what's open, what's done, and what's overdue.
🗓️
Registered Actions Calendar
A calendar view of all action plan due dates across your entire team. Switch between Day, Week, and Month views. Use it between coaching sessions to see what is coming up and catch anything that is about to go overdue before it becomes a problem.
🐛
Report an Issue — Not in use yet
This button is visible in the app but is not active during the current integration phase. Do not use it yet. All issues go directly to Evan Dharmasena. You will be told when in-app reporting is live.
Coming Soon
0
1
✅ Tip: Feedback is created from Weekly Development using the green button — the Feedback Tracker is a read-only history of what has been logged. Registered Actions follow the same principle — you create them, agents see them automatically.
Next up
Section 03
How to Use It ⏱ 4 min

How the GSO Feedback App fits into your day-to-day as a Team Leader.

1
Check your agents' performance data
Start in Weekly or Monthly Development. Select an agent and review their current CSAT, SQS, and AHT. Know the numbers before the coaching conversation. For backup coverage, use All Teams to access agents outside your regular roster.
2
Log weekly feedback
Stay in Weekly Development — you do not need to go anywhere else. Each week row has a green Add/see a review button. Click it to open the feedback form for that agent and week. Add your Operational Feedback (KPIs, patterns, coaching observations) and Quality Feedback (your observations on the quality of the agent's work). Choose whether to send it as an email. Save — it appears in the agent’s app immediately. Use the Feedback Tracker to view and track entries you have already logged.
3
Run coaching conversations
Use the app as your coaching base. Pull up DSAT cases in Your Team CSAT. Review SQS subscores. Show the agent their own trend charts. Make coaching specific and evidence-based — not just a conversation about a number.
4
Log agreed action plans
After the session, open Register an Action and log what was agreed. Set the task type, dates, status, and describe the activity clearly. The agent sees it immediately — only log what was genuinely discussed and agreed.
5
Track follow-through
Use the Registered Actions Board during check-ins to review open actions with the agent. Use the Calendar to see what's due across your whole team and stay ahead of anything going overdue.
6
Report issues to Evan Dharmasena
Do not use the Report an Issue button yet. Contact Evan Dharmasena directly for any technical problems — he is the current POC and will escalate to the app team on your behalf.
💡 Best practice: Log feedback the same day you do the review. Timely feedback is far more useful to agents than something written weeks after the fact.
Next up
Section 04
Your Metrics ⏱ 4 min

What the data in the app means — and how to use each metric to drive better coaching conversations.

MetricWhat it measuresWhat to do with it
CSAT %The percentage of customers who gave a positive rating after their support interactionDon't stop at the number. Go to Your Team CSAT and read the actual DSAT cases. The customer comment usually tells you exactly what went wrong.
DSATCases where the customer gave a score of 0 — actively dissatisfiedYour highest-value coaching cases. Review them in Your Team CSAT before every coaching session. Specific cases make for specific coaching.
SQSSupport Quality Score — the result of a QA audit, broken down into Communication, Case Handling, Compliance, Diagnosis Resolution, and Escalation TransferNever coach on the overall SQS alone. Drill into the subscores — a low Communication score needs a very different conversation than a low Compliance score.
AHTAverage Handling Time — how long the agent spends on each case on averageBoth extremes matter. Very high AHT may mean the agent is struggling. Very low AHT may mean they are rushing. Always read AHT alongside CSAT and SQS before drawing conclusions.
IRT Chat / Email %How often the agent responded to chat and email contacts within the required timeRed flags here usually indicate workload or prioritisation issues. Look for patterns — is it consistent or specific to certain days or weeks?
NE CRT% / CRT%How often customers contact again after a case is closed — a signal of whether the issue was actually resolvedHigh CRT often means the case was not truly resolved. Pair with CSAT comments to identify whether it is a knowledge gap, a communication issue, or something else.
OBC / AssignableOBC = Outbound Call Rate. Assignable = the proportion of time an agent spends in chat/email status (actively taking cases) versus backlog status. Agents should stay assignable — taking live cases rather than sitting idle.Low Assignable % means the agent is spending too much time in backlog status and not enough taking live cases. OBC gives context on outbound volume. Both are useful for workload and availability conversations.
Next up
Section 05
FAQs ⏱ 3 min

Common questions from Team Leads — answered clearly.

Yes. Data updates automatically and everything is managed inside the app. Check with your manager or Transformation Lead (Evan Dharmasena) if there are any transitional steps during go-live.
Operational Feedback is what you write as the Team Leader — your observations on KPIs like AHT, IRT compliance, adherence, and any patterns from your coaching. Quality Feedback is your assessment of the quality of an agent's work — case handling, communication, accuracy, and any quality observations from reviewing their interactions. It is not tied exclusively to a QA audit. Both fields appear in the same feedback form, accessed from Weekly Development via the green Add/see a review button. Once saved, the entry appears in the Feedback Tracker and in the agent's app.
Yes — the moment you save them. Only log what was genuinely agreed in your coaching session. Nothing should be a surprise to the agent.
The Board is agent-focused — select one agent and see all their actions by status. Use it during coaching check-ins. The Calendar is team-focused and time-based — it shows due dates across all your agents. Use it between sessions to stay ahead of anything going overdue.
Weekly Development — individual agent view, full drill-down, your regular team only. Team Performance — your whole team side by side, with threshold flagging. All Teams — for backup Team Leader situations only. Agents not on your roster only appear here.
Edit and delete functionality is still in development. If this is something you need, submit a request via the improvement ideas form — your input helps prioritise what gets built next.
The Report an Issue button is not active yet. Contact Evan Dharmasena directly with any technical issues. You will be notified when in-app reporting is available.
When you are covering for another Team Manager or acting in a backup capacity, use the All Teams view to access agents outside your regular roster — they will not appear in your Weekly or Monthly Development tabs. All the same tools apply: you can log feedback, create action plans, and monitor performance for those agents the same way you would for your own team. If you are unsure about the scope of your responsibilities during backup coverage, check with your manager or Transformation Lead (Evan Dharmasena).
When you choose to send feedback as an email, the agent receives a message showing their Key Performance Indicators for that period in a clear table format. The email is sent from the Operations Team and shows the agent's name, the period, and their KPIs including Survey Count, CSAT%, AHT, Response Received %, NE CRT%, OBC%, IRT Chat%, IRT Email%, Triage Chat%, Number of Audits, and Quality Score%.
